Title: The chromosome-scale assembly of the Salvia plebeia genome provides insight into the biosynthesis and regulation of rosmarinic acid

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Salvia plebeia; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Salvia plebeia; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive phenolic acid with food and pharmaceutical application potential
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by methyl jasmonate in hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by methyl jasmonate in hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate treatment; hairy root culture; transient expression in tobacco leaves
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Caffeoyl-CoA + salvianic acid A; caffeoyl-CoA + 4-hydroxyphenyllactic acid; p-coumaroyl-CoA + salvianic acid A; p-coumaroyl-CoA + 4-hydroxyphenyllactic acid
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Rosmarinic acid; caffeoyl-4'-hydroxyphenyllactic acid; 4-coumaroyl-3',4'-hydroxyphenyllactic acid; 4-coumaroyl-4'-hydroxyphenyllactic acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers hydroxycinnamoyl groups from acyl-CoA donors to phenyllactic acid derivatives, directly forming rosmarinic acid or its ester precursors
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product and forms immediate precursors
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes rosmarinic acid biosynthesis; provides direct enzymatic formation of rosmarinic acid and precursor esters
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic candidate selection; heterologous expression in E. coli; purified enzyme assay; HPLC/UPLC-Q-TOF-MS product identification; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly and annotation; comparative transcriptome analysis under MeJA treatment; BioProject PRJCA020085
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful metabolic engineering target for rosmarinic acid production; can be stacked with CYP98A genes and regulator SpbHLH54 for enhanced flux
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SpRAS was identified from 13 candidate RAS genes as the functional Salvia plebeia rosmarinic acid synthase. 2. SpRAS catalysed formation of rosmarinic acid from caffeoyl-CoA and salvianic acid A. 3. SpRAS also produced multiple rosmarinic acid precursors from alternative donor/acceptor combinations. 4. Co-expression with SpCYP98A genes in Nicotiana benthamiana supported its in vivo role in rosmarinic acid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SpRAS also contributes to precursor diversity, indicating substrate promiscuity relevant for synthetic biology.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Salvia plebeia; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Salvia plebeia; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive phenolic acid with food and pharmaceutical application potential
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Hydroxylation step of rosmarinic acid precursor branch
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 monooxygenase, CYP98A subfamily; contains PERF motif, heme-binding cysteine motif, threonine-containing binding pocket
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in tobacco leaves; yeast microsome assay
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates rosmarinic acid precursors at C-3 and/or C-3' positions, converting mono-hydroxylated or less hydroxylated esters into rosmarinic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes rosmarinic acid formation from precursor esters; bifunctional hydroxylase with stronger activity at one ring position than the others
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; expression in yeast WAT11; microsomal enzyme assay; UPLC-Q-TOF-MS product identification; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly and annotation; BioProject PRJCA020085
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising enzyme for synthetic biology and large-scale rosmarinic acid production, especially due to bifunctional hydroxylation capability
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SpCYP98A75 converts caffeoyl-4'-hydroxyphenyllactic acid and 4-coumaroyl-3',4'-hydroxyphenyllactic acid to rosmarinic acid. 2. It also converts 4-coumaroyl-4'-hydroxyphenyllactic acid into both 4-coumaroyl-3',4'-hydroxyphenyllactic acid and rosmarinic acid. 3. The enzyme is bifunctional, catalysing both C-3 and C-3' hydroxylation steps. 4. In vivo transient assays supported its role together with SpRAS in rosmarinic acid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Text contains inconsistent wording on positional preference: Summary says stronger at C-3', Results say stronger at C-3, while Discussion says stronger at C-3'. Bifunctionality is consistently supported.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Salvia plebeia; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Salvia plebeia; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive phenolic acid with food and pharmaceutical application potential
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Hydroxylation step of rosmarinic acid precursor branch
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 monooxygenase, CYP98A subfamily; contains PERF motif, heme-binding cysteine motif, threonine-containing binding pocket
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in tobacco leaves; yeast microsome assay
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates rosmarinic acid precursors, preferentially converting 4-coumaroyl-4'-hydroxyphenyllactic acid via C-3' hydroxylation and converting mono-hydroxylated precursors to rosmarinic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes rosmarinic acid formation from precursor esters
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; expression in yeast WAT11; microsomal enzyme assay; UPLC-Q-TOF-MS product identification; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly and annotation; BioProject PRJCA020085
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate enzyme for pathway engineering of rosmarinic acid production in heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SpCYP98A77 converts caffeoyl-4'-hydroxyphenyllactic acid and 4-coumaroyl-3',4'-hydroxyphenyllactic acid to rosmarinic acid. 2. It converts 4-coumaroyl-4'-hydroxyphenyllactic acid into caffeoyl-4'-hydroxyphenyllactic acid. 3. Transient expression with SpRAS in Nicotiana benthamiana supported in vivo contribution to rosmarinic acid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper contains inconsistency between Summary and Results/Discussion regarding whether SpCYP98A77/78 favor C-3 or C-3' hydroxylation; enzyme involvement in rosmarinic acid formation is clear.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species: Salvia plebeia; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Salvia plebeia; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly upregulated by methyl jasmonate in hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly upregulated by methyl jasmonate in hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate treatment; hairy root transformation; transient tobacco assay; yeast one-hybrid assay
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis of rosmarinic acid and homoplantaginin
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation via binding E-box cis-elements in target promoters
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates SpRAS and SpFNS promoters; overexpression also upregulates SpPAL, SpC4H, Sp4CL2, SpCHS1, SpCHI, SpFNS, SpF6H1, SpF6OMT2, SpUGT1 and SpRAS
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive JA-responsive regulator that directly activates key biosynthetic genes and substantially increases rosmarinic acid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases rosmarinic acid and homoplantaginin; RNAi knockdown decreases both metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative transcriptome under MeJA; hairy-root overexpression and RNAi; metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase transient assay
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (Renilla internal control in reporter system); constitutive SpbHLH54 effector expression in Dual-LUC
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative transcriptome analysis under MeJA treatment; genome-wide bHLH family analysis; BioProject PRJCA020085
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful regulatory target to coordinately enhance rosmarinic acid and homoplantaginin accumulation; can be combined with structural genes for metabolic engineering, but pleiotropic JA-related effects should be monitored
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SpbHLH54 was identified from MeJA-responsive TFs as a candidate regulator of rosmarinic acid biosynthesis. 2. Overexpression in hairy roots increased rosmarinic acid by 1.87–2.31-fold and homoplantaginin by 2.11–2.40-fold, whereas RNAi reduced both metabolites. 3. SpbHLH54 directly binds E-box elements in SpRAS and SpFNS promoters. 4. Dual-LUC assays confirmed transcriptional activation of SpRAS and SpFNS by SpbHLH54.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although many biosynthetic genes changed in transgenic lines, direct evidence was shown specifically for SpRAS and SpFNS.
